title: ChatGPT Pro Plugin vs Codex GPT Bridge

sections.title: 结论
sections.summary: 日常 Codex 调 ChatGPT 时，外部 plugin 是目标线但未过启用门槛；本 repo 继续做反向 MCP 桥。

sections.title: 全方位对比表
sections.summary: 按方向、MCP、浏览器、上下文、安全、状态和稳定性拆开。

sections.title: 融会贯通方案
sections.summary: 保留两条链路，各自负责最强场景。

sections.title: 日常流程
sections.summary: 给 Codex 调 ChatGPT 和 ChatGPT 调 Codex 两套固定用法。

sections.title: 证据与验证
sections.summary: 列出已查证的源码、命令、失败点和限制。

components.purpose: 概括方向、主链路、保留资产和当前阻塞。
把采用、保留和暂缓项分开。
定义外部 plugin 升级为日常默认前必须通过的门槛。
说明未验证和残余风险。

claims.text: For the user's Codex -> ChatGPT Pro website goal, codex-chatgpt-pro-plugin is the better target line than the API-backed ask_chatgpt path, but it should not be treated as the daily default until install, packaging, and live proof gates pass.
codex-gpt-bridge remains the better path for ChatGPT UI -> local Codex MCP execution.
The external plugin is not a replacement for Local Codex Bridge Secure because it is a Codex plugin/CLI controlling ChatGPT Web UI, not a ChatGPT-facing MCP server.
The external plugin currently needs compatibility/packaging fixes before being treated as production-ready because plugin-install smoke and materialized package init both expose local failures.

evidence.summary: External README says the plugin drives a logged-in ChatGPT Pro browser session, attaches repo context, reads the answer, and records receipts.
External browser contract documents a dedicated Chrome profile and CDP endpoint at 127.0.0.1:9222.
External package exposes chatgpt-pro CLI and a Codex skill, not a ChatGPT-facing MCP server.
Local clone at commit 0cc5c550 failed npm test at test:plugin-install because current Codex CLI rejected plugin marketplace add ... --json.
Materialized plugin bin help ran, but init failed because plugins/codex-chatgpt-pro-plugin/.codex/skills/chatgpt-pro-line/SKILL.md was missing.
evidence.source: node /tmp/codex-chatgpt-pro-plugin-current/plugins/codex-chatgpt-pro-plugin/bin/chatgpt-pro init in a temporary directory

evidence.summary: README documents ChatGPT -> Codex via Local Codex Bridge Secure and Codex -> ChatGPT-like model via OpenAI Responses API.
src/tools.ts registers bridge_status, codex_read, codex_run, codex_reply, and codex_job_status with read-only defaults and sandbox gates.
src/chatgptMcp.ts implements ask_chatgpt through the OpenAI Responses API and states it is not ChatGPT Web UI.
npm run check passed in codex-gpt-bridge with TypeScript build and 35 Vitest tests.

checks.name: external repository clone
checks.detail: Cloned pauljunsukhan/codex-chatgpt-pro-plugin to /tmp and inspected commit 0cc5c5509ebaccfc7dad45b9e02a0f86407c817c.

checks.name: external deterministic test
checks.detail: npm test failed at test:plugin-install because Codex CLI rejected --json on plugin marketplace add.

checks.name: external materialized package init smoke
checks.detail: The materialized plugin bin could show help, but init failed because its .codex/skills path was absent.

checks.name: external focused no-live selftests
checks.detail: test:repo-context-policy, test:non-interference, and test:cli-init passed without touching a live ChatGPT login.

checks.name: bridge test suite
checks.detail: npm run check passed: build plus 4 Vitest files / 35 tests.

checks.name: adversarial review
checks.detail: Independent reviewer flagged that the report overstated immediate daily adoption. The report was revised to make chatgpt-pro a target line gated by install, packaging, and live proof.

limitations: No live ChatGPT Pro browser call was run in this comparison; website automation stability is inferred from source and tests, not from a fresh live session.
External plugin is young and public repo currently has no releases; adoption should start as companion/fork/wrapper, not as a hard dependency inside this bridge.
Generated repo context has a secret scan, but explicit upload files still need caller-side scrubbing and review.

ChatGPT Web UI automation can break when the website DOM or model selector changes. The plugin mitigates this with probes, receipts, and fail-closed errors, but cannot make it as stable as an official API.
The current bridge's Codex -> ChatGPT path uses the OpenAI Responses API and may incur API usage cost; it does not access ChatGPT Pro website-only behavior.
